pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage introducing the researcher and providing quick access to key sections, driven by profile settings and summary data.
primary_functions: Provide a concise overview of the researcher, tagline, and key research areas.
Highlight a featured publication, project, and upcoming talk based on profile display settings.
Offer clear entry points to Publications, Projects, Courses, Talks, Contact, and Settings pages.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Publications' header link or a 'View all publications' button.
Click on the featured publication card.
Click on the featured project card.
Click on the next upcoming event card.
Click the 'Projects' header link or 'Explore research projects' button.
Click the 'Courses' header link or teaching teaser.
Click the 'Talks & Events' header link.
Click a 'Contact / Supervision' call-to-action on the home page.
Click a 'Edit profile' or 'Profile settings' button visible only to the owner.

access_methods.description: Primary landing page accessible via the 'Home' header link.
Default URL when visiting the root of the site.

pages.name: Publications
pages.description: Master list of all research publications with rich filtering, sorting, and list-saving tools supporting reading list creation and citation-centric workflows.
primary_functions: Search and filter publications by keyword, publication type (e.g., journal article, conference paper), year range, and citation count.
Sort results by criteria such as citations (high to low) and year (newest first).
Select one or more publications and add them to a named reading list (create new list or add to existing).
Navigate to individual publication detail pages for full metadata and citation tools.

incoming_params.description: Initial search keyword, e.g., 'reinforcement learning' or 'graph neural network'.
Preselected publication type filter value, e.g., 'journal_article' or 'conference_paper'.
Lower bound of publication year range filter.
Upper bound of publication year range filter.
Minimum citation count filter for publications.
Sort order key, e.g., 'citations_high_to_low' or 'year_newest_first'.
Current page number for paginated results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a publication title in the results list.
After adding selected publications to a reading list, click 'View list'.
Click a 'Create note for this publication' shortcut (if provided) on a result row.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Publications' header link.
Linked from Home and other sections for exploring publications.

pages.name: Publication Detail
pages.description: Detailed view for a single publication including metadata, metrics, citation tools, and links to related research objects.
primary_functions: Display full publication information, including authors, venue, year, abstract, and citation counts.
Provide citation tools with multiple styles (e.g., APA) and a one-click copy-to-clipboard control.
Allow adding the publication directly to existing or new reading lists.
Show related items such as associated projects or talks for additional exploration.

incoming_params.description: ID of the publication to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Save citation to notes' or 'Open in Notes' control after copying an APA citation.
Click on a related project card in the 'Related projects' section.
Click on a related talk/event in the 'Related talks & events' section.
Click a 'Back to all publications' link.

access_methods.description: Accessed via links that supply a publication 'id' query parameter.
Linked from publication lists, projects, events, and saved lists.

pages.name: Research Projects
pages.description: Listing of research projects with search, filtering, and access to detailed project information, including funding and team data.
primary_functions: Search and filter projects by keyword (e.g., 'climate modeling') and other attributes such as status or topic.
Display key project summary fields such as funding amount and team size in the list.
Allow opening project detail pages (including in new tabs) for deeper inspection.
Provide quick access to bookmark controls or indicate which projects are already bookmarked.

incoming_params.description: Keyword search term, e.g., 'climate modeling'.
Preselected project status filter, e.g., 'active' or 'completed'.
Sort order key, e.g., 'funding_high_to_low'.
Page number for paginated project results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a project title in the search results (including Ctrl-click for new tab).
After bookmarking a project, click 'Manage bookmarks' in the confirmation UI.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Projects' header link.
Linked from Home, publications, and talks when exploring related projects.

pages.name: Project Detail
pages.description: Detailed page for a single research project, including funding, team composition, and related research outputs.
primary_functions: Display comprehensive project information including description, objectives, funding amount, and team members list.
Allow users to bookmark/save the project and organize it into folders (e.g., 'Potential collaborations').
Show links to related publications, talks, or datasets associated with the project.
Indicate whether the project is currently bookmarked and in which folder(s).

incoming_params.description: ID of the project to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: After clicking 'Bookmark' and choosing a folder such as 'Potential collaborations', click 'View in bookmarks'.
Click a related publication in the 'Related publications' section.
Click a related talk/event in the 'Related events' section.
Click a 'Back to projects' link.

access_methods.description: Accessed via links that include a project 'id' query parameter.
Opened from project listings, bookmarks, and saved lists.

pages.name: Teaching & Courses
pages.description: List of courses taught by the researcher with filters for term, level, and credits, supporting creation of personal course lists.
primary_functions: Filter courses by term or semester (e.g., Spring 2025), course level (undergraduate/graduate), and credits or units.
Sort courses by course number or other criteria to control ordering.
Select one or more courses and save them into named course lists (e.g., 'Courses to Take 2025') by creating or choosing a list.
Provide course summaries (title, number, term, credits) and optional navigation to more detailed course information if present.

incoming_params.description: Initial term filter, e.g., 'Spring 2025'.
Initial course level filter, e.g., 'graduate'.
Minimum credits filter for courses.
Sort order key, e.g., 'course_number_low_to_high'.
Page number for paginated course results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: After selecting courses and saving them to a list, click 'View course list'.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Courses' header link.
Linked from the Home page teaching teaser or other internal content.

pages.name: Talks & Events
pages.description: Overview of upcoming and past talks/events with filtering and sorting tools for discovering relevant public events.
primary_functions: Filter events by date range (e.g., today to six months ahead), location (e.g., New York, Online), and access type (e.g., public).
Sort events by date (soonest first) to identify the earliest upcoming relevant talk.
Display event summaries including title, date, location, and access type, with links to event detail pages.
Provide quick actions such as saving an event to an Attend list where appropriate.

incoming_params.description: Start of the date range filter (YYYY-MM-DD).
End of the date range filter (YYYY-MM-DD).
Location filter, e.g., 'New York' or 'Online'.
Access type filter, e.g., 'public'.
Sort order key, e.g., 'date_soonest_first'.
Page number for paginated event results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an event title in the list (including the earliest upcoming event).

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Talks & Events' header link.
Linked from the Home page and related project/publication pages.

pages.name: Event Detail
pages.description: Detailed information page for a single talk or event, including scheduling and Attend-list integration.
primary_functions: Show full event details including title, abstract, date and time, location (including whether it is online), and access information.
Allow saving the event to an Attend list or other named event lists (create new or select existing).
Provide reminder/notification settings, including selecting a reminder time such as '3 days before' and confirming it.
Link back to the overall Talks & Events list and to related publications or projects if available.

incoming_params.description: ID of the event to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: After adding the event to an Attend list, click 'View Attend list'.
Click 'View related projects' from the related projects section.
Click a specific related project card.
Click a related publication associated with this event.
Click 'Back to all talks & events'.

access_methods.description: Accessed via links that include an event 'id' query parameter.
Opened from the Talks & Events list, Home, projects, publications, and saved lists.

pages.name: Notes & Clipboard
pages.description: Central place to store, edit, and review notes such as copied citations or supervision ideas, functioning as a personal clipboard.
primary_functions: Create new notes with a title and rich-text or plain-text content (e.g., pasting APA citations).
Edit or delete existing notes to keep the clipboard organized.
Optionally tag or associate notes with specific publications, projects, or events for context.
Serve as the target page when accessing the 'Notes' or 'Clipboard' feature from the header.

incoming_params.description: Optional publication ID to pre-associate with a new note (e.g., when saving a citation).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a link from a note that is associated with a specific publication.
Click 'Manage saved lists' from the Notes page sidebar.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Notes' header link.
Linked from citation tools and other pages where notes are useful.

pages.name: Saved Lists
pages.description: Management hub for all user-defined lists such as reading lists, course lists, and attend lists, with item-level navigation.
primary_functions: Display all existing lists (e.g., 'Thesis Sources', 'Courses to Take 2025', 'Attend') with their types and item counts.
Allow viewing the contents of a specific list and removing items from it.
Support renaming or deleting entire lists when they are no longer needed.
Provide navigation back to the originating content (publications, courses, events) for items in each list.

incoming_params.description: ID of a specific saved list to open by default.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a publication item inside a reading list such as 'Thesis Sources'.
From a course list such as 'Courses to Take 2025', click 'Find similar courses'.
Click on an event inside an Attend list.
From a project-type list, click 'Browse related projects'.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Saved Lists' footer link.
Opened from dialogs after creating or updating a saved list.
Specific lists are opened via a 'listId' query parameter.

pages.name: Bookmarks
pages.description: Page for viewing and organizing bookmarked items, especially research projects, grouped into folders.
primary_functions: List all bookmarked projects and optionally other bookmarked entities (e.g., events) in one place.
Organize bookmarks into folders such as 'Potential collaborations' and allow creating, renaming, or deleting folders.
Move bookmarked items between folders or remove them from bookmarks.
Provide quick navigation from each bookmark back to its detail page.

incoming_params.description: Optional folder ID to focus the view on a specific bookmark folder.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a bookmarked project in any folder (e.g., 'Potential collaborations').
Click on a bookmarked event.
From a publication-type bookmark, click 'Search similar publications'.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Bookmarks' footer link.
Linked from project and event pages after bookmarking.
Specific folders are focused via a 'folderId' query parameter.

pages.name: Contact & Supervision
pages.description: Contact page with a form for general inquiries and structured supervision requests, including an availability scheduler.
primary_functions: Provide a contact form with fields for name, email, subject, reason for contact (e.g., prospective PhD student), and message.
Include an embedded scheduling or availability picker to select preferred meeting times (e.g., earliest Wednesday 10:00–12:00).
Validate and submit supervision or contact requests so they are sent to the researcher.
Display contact-related information such as response expectations or office hours.

incoming_params.description: Preselected contact reason, e.g., 'prospective_phd_student' or 'collaboration_request'.
Pre-filled subject line for the contact form.

outgoing_connections.trigger: After submitting the form, click a 'Back to Home' or 'Return to profile' button.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Contact' header link.
Also accessible via the 'Contact & Supervision' footer link.
Linked from project pages (e.g., 'Contact about this project') and the About page.

pages.name: Profile & Display Settings
pages.description: Single settings page for editing profile content (tagline and keywords) and public display options for publications.
primary_functions: Allow editing of the profile tagline or short bio line and saving changes.
Manage primary research keywords/tags by adding, removing, or replacing them, ensuring only the desired three primary keywords remain.
Configure public publication display settings, including the number of publications to show (e.g., limit to 5) and sorting order (e.g., citations high to low).
Select a single featured publication from post-2021 items (e.g., filter to 2022+ and choose one), and ensure only that publication is marked as featured before saving settings.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View public profile' or similar after saving settings to see updated tagline and featured publication on Home.
Click 'Open publications with this ordering' from the publications display settings section.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Profile settings' header link (for the site owner).

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background and biography page for the researcher, presenting narrative context and research themes.
primary_functions: Present a narrative biography including academic background, research themes, and career overview.
Summarize key accomplishments and positions in a human-readable format complementary to the CV.
Provide links to other relevant sections such as Publications, Projects, and CV.
Offer context for visitors about the researcher’s broader interests and activities.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'View full CV' link.
Click 'Explore publications' from the About page.
Click 'Explore research projects' from the About page.
Click a 'Get in touch' call-to-action.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About' footer link.
May be linked from external profiles or institutional pages.

pages.name: Curriculum Vitae
pages.description: Dedicated page for viewing and downloading the researcher’s CV, structured into sections.
primary_functions: Display an embedded or formatted version of the current CV for in-browser reading.
Offer a downloadable file (e.g., PDF) of the CV for offline use.
Provide quick links back to About, Publications, and other relevant sections.
Optionally highlight selected sections of the CV such as education, positions, and awards.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to About' link from the CV page.
Click 'See publications' near the publications section of the CV.
Click 'See research projects' near the research section of the CV.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Curriculum Vitae' footer link.
Often linked directly from external sites or emails.

header_links.description: All research publications with filters and reading lists
Teaching and courses list with filters
Upcoming and past talks and events
Notes and clipboard for saved citations and ideas
Contact and supervision request form with scheduler
Edit profile tagline, research keywords, and public display settings

footer_links.description: Manage reading lists, course lists, and attend list
View and organize bookmarked projects and other items
